source_policy: Current official Channel Islands model pages and governed canonical manufacturer descriptions are authoritative. Public family follows manufacturer design intent. Board DNA describes behaviour but does not determine identity.

models.manufacturer_intent: Simple, fun and easy board for maximising wave count and speed in weak surf.
models.editorial_reason: Weak-wave ease and planing performance are primary, so it belongs with Grovellers rather than Daily Drivers.

models.manufacturer_intent: An extension of the Happy Everyday designed to be ridden even more often across everyday conditions.
models.editorial_reason: Everyday versatility is explicit and central.

models.manufacturer_intent: Performance-oriented board for older or larger surfers needing added paddle and support.
models.editorial_reason: Broad usability and support make it a Daily Driver, while retaining performance DNA.

models.manufacturer_intent: Very short quad created to maximise fun and performance in tiny summer surf.
models.editorial_reason: Tiny-wave purpose is primary, so it belongs in Grovellers.

models.manufacturer_intent: World Tour performance board for poor-to-fair surf and dynamic rail or aerial surfing.
models.editorial_reason: Competition performance remains the primary design role despite its lower-end range.

models.manufacturer_intent: Stab in the Dark-winning high-performance design tuned for elite surfing.
models.editorial_reason: Competition and elite performance intent are explicit.

models.manufacturer_intent: Next-generation fish with traditional lineage and added performance across grovel and clean points.
models.editorial_reason: Fish lineage and outline remain the primary identity.

models.manufacturer_intent: Modern classic egg-inspired mid-length focused on longer-railed lines, glide and flow.
models.editorial_reason: Mid-length identity is explicit and central.

models.manufacturer_intent: Twin-fin evolution of the CI Mid retaining mid-length glide with a freer feel.
models.editorial_reason: The longer rail line and mid-length platform remain primary.

models.manufacturer_intent: Highly rockered Stab in the Dark-winning board described as the pinnacle of high performance.
models.editorial_reason: This is a clear elite Performance Shortboard, not a Daily Driver.

models.manufacturer_intent: Short, wide step-down created to make weak summer surf highly surfable while retaining performance.
models.editorial_reason: Small-wave optimisation is the primary role.

models.manufacturer_intent: Modernised Dumpster Diver with more rail line and performance for weak surf.
models.editorial_reason: It remains a small-wave specialist despite increased performance range.

models.manufacturer_intent: Accessible tour-level performance shortboard rooted in proven Al Merrick high-performance designs.
models.editorial_reason: High-performance intent is explicit.

models.manufacturer_intent: Fast, skatey and responsive swallow-tail hybrid built for fun and broad use.
models.editorial_reason: Versatile hybrid intent is stronger than fish identity or strict HPSB purpose.

models.manufacturer_intent: All-round shortboard for above-average surf with aggressive tail curve and rail-focused performance.
models.editorial_reason: Quality-wave performance is primary, so it should not sit in Daily Drivers.

models.manufacturer_intent: Heavy-wave and barrel board proven at Pipeline and designed to maximise time in steep, powerful surf.
models.editorial_reason: Powerful-wave barrel intent makes it a Step Up, not a Daily Driver.

models.manufacturer_intent: Performance shortboard for speed, critical surfing and tube performance.
models.editorial_reason: Performance purpose is explicit.

models.manufacturer_intent: Everyday shortboard derived from Two Happy for a wider range of conditions.
models.editorial_reason: The manufacturer explicitly defines it as an everyday board.

models.manufacturer_intent: Peter Mel and Britt Merrick big-wave gun designed for speed, control and user-friendly paddling at Mavericks.
models.editorial_reason: It is explicitly a big-wave gun and must be governed under Step Up.

models.manufacturer_intent: Return to Mikey February's high-performance shortboarding roots and precision surfing.
models.editorial_reason: High-performance shortboard identity is explicit.

models.manufacturer_intent: Dane Reynolds small-wave design refined for speed, release and progressive surfing.
models.editorial_reason: Its main role is progressive performance in weaker waves.

models.manufacturer_intent: Classic solution for more speed and flow in lesser waves while maintaining world-tour-level performance.
models.editorial_reason: Broad everyday range and performance balance make it a Daily Driver.

models.manufacturer_intent: Wide, fast board built to carry speed and perform in weaker surf.
models.editorial_reason: Small-wave speed generation is the primary role.

models.manufacturer_intent: World Tour-tested contest workhorse designed for heat-winning performance.
models.editorial_reason: Competition intent outweighs its wider, more accessible outline.

models.manufacturer_intent: Shorter, wider step-down for smaller or weaker surf while preserving shortboard performance.
models.editorial_reason: It sits between fishy boards and a normal shortboard, making it a hybrid Daily Driver, not Fish.

models.manufacturer_intent: Performance board with forward volume for easier paddle and wave entry while retaining technical surfing capability.
models.editorial_reason: Its design remains performance-first rather than everyday-first.

models.manufacturer_intent: Shorter, wider modern Flyer designed to revolutionise high-performance surfing in small waves.
models.editorial_reason: Small-wave performance is the core role, placing it with Grovellers.

models.manufacturer_intent: Fast, powerful all-round high-performance shortboard for maximum speed and control.
models.editorial_reason: It remains a high-performance shortboard; stronger-wave capability is secondary.

models.manufacturer_intent: Dane Reynolds' advanced-surfer board for good, pockety surf with continuous rocker.
models.editorial_reason: Good-wave technical performance is primary, not everyday versatility.

models.manufacturer_intent: Specialist board developed for serious larger surf and committed wave entry.
models.editorial_reason: The model's core role is powerful, consequential surf.

models.manufacturer_intent: Modernised twin-fin rooted in classic fast, loose alternative surfing.
models.editorial_reason: Twin-fin fish lineage and planing feel are primary.

models.manufacturer_intent: Mikey February performance twin-pin for speed, hold and quality-wave surfing.
models.editorial_reason: Quality-wave performance and hold distinguish it from fish-first twins.

models.manufacturer_intent: High-performance Happy evolution made more accessible across surfers and conditions.
models.editorial_reason: Accessibility does not change its primary high-performance identity.

models.manufacturer_intent: Small-to-medium all-round board intended to cover a broad everyday role.
models.editorial_reason: All-round versatility makes Daily Driver the correct public family.
